citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
While performing record review, it was discovered that the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) failed to
review the program for humane care and use of animals when required in June and December 2025. This was confirmed
by a facility representative. Failure to complete the semiannual program review as required by the Animal Welfare Act
may delay the identification and correction of program deficiencies, welfare concerns, discrepancies, or other issues that
could negatively impact the health and well-being of animals covered under the program. With respect to activities
involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, must review, at least once every six months, the
research facility’s program for humane care and use of animals, using title 9, chapter I, subchapter A – Animal Welfare, as
a basis for evaluation.
Correct by: March 27, 2026

While performing record review it was discovered that the facility did not complete and submit an annual report to the
Deputy Administrator since October 2024. This was confirmed by a facility representative. Failure to complete and submit
the annual report can negatively impact the health and well-being of the animals by undermining institutional and
regulatory oversight, which can result in inadequate identification and correction of welfare concerns. Each reporting
facility shall submit an annual report to the Deputy Administrator on or before December 1 of each calendar year. The
report shall cover the previous Federal fiscal year.
Correct by: March 27, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(1) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
While performing record review, it was discovered that the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) failed to 
review the program for humane care and use of animals when required in June and December 2025. This was confirmed 
by a facility representative. Failure to complete the semiannual program review as required by the Animal Welfare Act 
may delay the identification and correction of program deficiencies, welfare concerns, discrepancies, or other issues that 
could negatively impact the health and well-being of animals covered under the program. With respect to activities 
involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, must review, at least once every six months, the 
research facility’s program for humane care and use of animals, using title 9, chapter I, subchapter A – Animal Welfare, as 
a basis for evaluation.  
 
Correct by: March 27, 2026 
2.36(a) 
   
Annual report. 
While performing record review it was discovered that the facility did not complete and submit an annual report to the 
Deputy Administrator since October 2024. This was confirmed by a facility representative. Failure to complete and submit 
the annual report can negatively impact the health and well-being of the animals by undermining institutional and 
regulatory oversight, which can result in inadequate identification and correction of welfare concerns. Each reporting 
facility shall submit an annual report to the Deputy Administrator on or before December 1 of each calendar year. The 
report shall cover the previous Federal fiscal year.  
 
Correct by: March 27, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
